name: radiology_queue_ai_contrast_gap
premise: Dr. Hannah Wexler, head of radiology at Ormond Medical, must decide whether to keep an AI queue-prioritization tool active during a weekend stroke backlog. The tool has reduced time-to-read for critical scans by 27% and helps compensate for two radiologists out sick. Fellow radiologist Dr. Arjun Mehta finds that the tool deprioritizes certain post-contrast scans from patients with kidney impairment because their image patterns resemble lower-risk follow-ups in the training data. Those patients are few, but delayed reads can miss early vascular complications. Disabling the tool will lengthen the queue for all stroke and trauma scans. Keeping it active improves average performance but may bury a small group whose scans require urgent human interpretation despite lower model confidence.

agents.name: Dr. Hannah Wexler
agents.identity: I am the head of radiology responsible for triage quality and imaging turnaround during staff shortages.
agents.goal: I want urgent scans read quickly without letting AI prioritization hide vulnerable subgroups.

agents.name: Dr. Arjun Mehta
agents.identity: I am the radiologist who discovered the kidney-impairment contrast-scan prioritization gap.
agents.goal: I want the queue process corrected before a small subgroup is harmed by delayed interpretation.

choices: I will disable the AI queue tool and return to manual triage, accepting slower average reads across the department.
I will keep the AI queue tool active and manually flag known kidney-impairment contrast scans, accepting that some affected cases may not be identified.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Dr. Wexler must decide whether to preserve average scan turnaround or disable a queue tool with a hidden subgroup blindspot. What does Dr. Hannah Wexler do?
